prompt: I have COVID case rate per 100k people per US county for 2020-2023, and I want to make a choropleth map for an article showing 'America's hardest-hit counties'. Should I use Jenks natural breaks or quantiles for the color scale? My intuition is Jenks but my editor says quantiles look more 'honest'. Which is right?

assertions.text: Gives a decisive recommendation between Jenks and quantiles (not just 'it depends')
Explains what each scheme does perceptually and how it shapes the story the map tells
Raises at least one alternative to a pre-classed choropleth (proportional symbols, dot density, dasymetric, bivariate, or hex bins) OR notes the loss of quantitative resolution when pre-classing
Addresses the skewed distribution of county case rates (log scale, outlier handling, or trimming the legend)
Notes the communication-vs-discovery framing (for an *article*, the goal is communication, which sometimes warrants Jenks's natural-break narrative)
